name: PA Coach Lines
recent_news: 2021: Received >$800K PA DEP grant to upgrade buses to cleaner diesel. 2018-2022: Multiple legal disputes with McKeesport Area SD over busing contract; also litigation with Elizabeth Forward SD over service obligations.
fit_notes: Independent founder-led PA school bus contractor (~$5-13M rev, ~125 staff) in Summit's PA footprint serving multiple Allegheny/Westmoreland districts. Potential tuck-in but recent litigation with customer districts and possible relocation are diligence flags. Founder Sunstein could be a succession candidate.

name: Edward Schaffer & Son
recent_news: Defunct. Schaefer/Schaffer & Sons bus company (former East Hampton, NY school bus contractor at 41 Route 114) went out of business in 2006 just weeks before the school year, forcing East Hampton UFSD to take school transportation in-house. District later leased the former Schaefer site and built its own bus depot in 2021.
fit_notes: NOT a viable target - operating company appears defunct since 2006. Listing reflects stale data. The 631-324-1880 number is in the East Hampton, NY area code/exchange, consistent with the defunct Schaefer East Hampton operator. Skip.

name: Staten Island Bus Inc
recent_news: No recent public news found. Address 52 Bayview Ave, Staten Island, NY 10309 (Charleston/Tottenville area) is consistent with a yellow school bus depot. Not appearing on the NYCSBUS list or in news coverage of Reliant/Beacon/First Student acquisitions.
fit_notes: Possible small independent NYC OPT school bus contractor on Staten Island (~240 employees per source data). Public web footprint is near-zero, suggesting privately held legacy operator - typical Summit tuck-in profile. Needs primary diligence to confirm operating status, fleet, district contracts, and ownership; manager Michael Pompeo is the only identified principal.

name: Carefor Bus Co
recent_news: Pre-2020: Pioneered XL Hybrid retrofit on Type-A school buses, achieving ~25% fuel economy gain and 20% CO2 reduction; publicly recognized as one of NYC's earliest hybrid school bus operators.
fit_notes: Strong tuck-in candidate: family-owned (Hoffman) NYC OPT special-needs/Type-A operator since 1971, ~$8M revenue/~200 employees, with a recognizable brand and innovative hybrid fleet credentials. Multi-generational ownership (Neil + Marty Hoffman) suggests potential succession opportunity. NOTE: The 'Carefor' spelling in the source list is a typo for 'Careful Bus Service Inc.' (same address, very similar phone).

name: Cupie Transportation Corp
recent_news: Listed as CLOSED on Yelp as of November 2025. 145 Wolcott St, Brooklyn (Red Hook) address now associated with 21st Ave Bus Corp. and A&S School Transportation, suggesting Cupie's operations were wound down or absorbed by other Wolcott Street operators. Appeared on the Empire State Transportation WC Trust member listing (now terminated).
fit_notes: NOT a viable target as an operating company - Cupie Transportation listed CLOSED; depot at 145 Wolcott St now houses 21st Ave Bus Corp. and A&S School Transportation. If Summit wants the Brooklyn footprint, those successor operators are the better targets. Skip Cupie as an independent acquisition opportunity.
